content: I run a mid-size Shopify store and we get 50-100 support tickets daily. My team of 3 support staff is overwhelmed. Most tickets are repetitive: where is my order, I want a refund, etc. I need to automate the first-response layer so humans only handle complex cases.

**Phase 1: Deep Interview | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

That is a classic support automation use case. 50-100 tickets daily with mostly repetitive inquiries is exactly the kind of workflow that benefits from an agent team.

Let me start with the ticket flow. Where do tickets come in? Shopify inbox, email, or both? And how does your team currently prioritize them?

context: State bar present with correct phase and gate format
Tech level correctly detected as technical-basics

content: Tickets arrive via email (forwarded to a shared Gmail inbox) and Shopify inbox. No prioritization system. Agents just grab the next unread ticket. Order tracking inquiries are about 60% of volume, refund requests about 25%, and the rest are complex cases or complaints.

**Phase 1: Deep Interview | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

60% order tracking, 25% refunds, 15% complex. That distribution tells me we can automate roughly 85% of your ticket volume with the right classification.

For the refund flow: what are your refund policies? Is there a threshold amount? Do you process refunds through Stripe directly?

Refunds under EUR 50, support staff process directly in Stripe. Over EUR 50, they escalate to me via Slack for approval. Our return policy is 30 days, no questions asked for unused items. Used items are case-by-case.

**Phase 1: Deep Interview | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

Clear refund tiers: under EUR 50 auto-processable, over EUR 50 needs your approval via Slack. 30-day no-questions return policy with case-by-case for used items.

What tools does the team use day-to-day? I see Shopify, Gmail, Stripe, Slack. Anything else?

Those are the main ones. We also use Google Sheets to track refunds and their reasons, which helps us spot product quality issues. Shopify Plus plan so we have full API access. Stripe standard account.

**Phase 1: Deep Interview | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

Good. Tools confirmed: Shopify (Plus, full API), Stripe, Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets for refund tracking.

How many people are involved in the support workflow? Who handles what?

Me as the store owner, I approve large refunds and handle escalations. Three support staff handle everything else. They will focus on complex cases only once automation is in place. Customers receive responses via email.

**Phase 1: Deep Interview | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

Clear team structure. You (owner) handle escalations and approvals. Three staff transition to complex-only.

Now for data classification. Your system handles customer names, email addresses, shipping addresses, phone numbers (PII). It also processes payment amounts, refund amounts, and the last 4 digits of payment methods (financial data). Since you serve EU customers, GDPR is mandatory. The financial/payment card data also triggers PCI-DSS awareness: we need to ensure no full card numbers are ever stored or logged.

What about edge cases? Partial refunds, multi-shipment orders, duplicate tickets?

context: Data classification identified with PII and financial tags
PCI compliance activated for payment data

content: All of those happen. Partial refunds when only some items are returned. Orders with multiple shipments at different stages. Customers sometimes submit duplicate tickets about the same order. Also, customers occasionally reply to automated emails expecting a human.

**Phase 1: Deep Interview | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

All noted. Partial refunds, multi-shipment tracking, duplicate detection, and the expectation-management issue with automated replies. Those are critical edge cases for the design.

Reporting preferences: how do you want to be kept informed? Daily summaries? Real-time alerts?
Daily ticket summary to me via Slack. Weekly refund report in Google Sheets. Real-time Slack alerts for escalated tickets so I can act quickly.

**Phase 1: Deep Interview | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

Here is my complete understanding:

- **Problem:** 3 support staff overwhelmed by 50-100 daily tickets, 85% repetitive
- **Services:** Shopify (Plus), Stripe, Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets
- **Ticket types:** Order tracking (60%), refunds (25%), complex/escalation (15%)
- **Refund policy:** Under EUR 50 auto-process, over EUR 50 owner approval via Slack
- **Data:** PII (names, emails, addresses) + Financial (payments, refunds). GDPR + PCI-DSS awareness.
- **Edge cases:** Partial refunds, multi-shipment orders, duplicate tickets, automated reply confusion
- **Reporting:** Daily Slack summary, weekly refund sheet, real-time escalation alerts
- **Budget:** Minimal hosting budget. Go-live target: 2 weeks.

Does this capture everything?
Perfect summary. Approved.

**Phase 2: General Design | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

Phase 1 approved. Moving to agent team design.

Your support workflow has a natural routing pattern: tickets arrive, get classified, then different specialists handle each type. This maps to a **Hierarchy** topology with a coordinator agent.

**Topology: Hierarchy**

| Agent | Role | Blast Radius | Model | Trigger |
|-------|------|-------------|-------|--------|
| Support Coordinator | Classify tickets and route to specialists | L1: read-only | Sonnet | Event (new ticket) |
| Order Tracker | Look up order and shipping status, draft response | L2: write-scoped | Sonnet | Routed by Coordinator |
| Refund Processor | Evaluate refund eligibility, process or escalate | L3: write-unrestricted | Opus | Routed by Coordinator |
| Escalation Handler | Route complex cases to human team via Slack | L4: send-external | Sonnet | Routed by Coordinator |

```
              [Support Coordinator]
                   L1:read
                 /     |     \
  [Order Tracker] [Refund Processor] [Escalation Handler]
     L2:write        L3:write           L4:send
     Sonnet          Opus               Sonnet
```

Key decisions:
- **Hierarchy, not pipeline:** Tickets get routed to the right specialist immediately, instead of flowing through every agent sequentially.
- **Coordinator is read-only (L1):** It only reads tickets and classifies. Zero write risk.
- **Opus for Refund Processor:** Evaluating refund eligibility, partial refund calculations, and return policy edge cases requires reasoning. Other agents do structured lookups.
- **4 agents for 3 ticket types:** The coordinator is a separate concern (routing vs. execution).

Does this topology work for your team?

context: Hierarchy topology specified in Phase 2 design
Agent table present with proper headers
Blast radius levels assigned to agents
Coordinator shown in hierarchy diagram

content: Makes sense. Four agents with a coordinator routing to three specialists. Approved.

**Phase 3: Deep Integration Analysis | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

Phase 2 approved. Researching integrations for each agent.

### Support Coordinator Integrations

| Service | Method | Trust | Fallback |
|---------|--------|-------|----------|
| Shopify | Shopify MCP (community, 600+ stars) | HIGH | Shopify Admin API direct |
| Gmail | Google Workspace MCP (taylorwilsdon) | HIGH | Gmail API direct |

### Order Tracker Integrations

| Service | Method | Trust | Fallback |
|---------|--------|-------|----------|
| Shopify | Shopify MCP | HIGH | Shopify Admin API |
| Gmail (send response) | Google Workspace MCP | HIGH | Gmail API |

### Refund Processor Integrations

| Service | Method | Trust | Fallback |
|---------|--------|-------|----------|
| Stripe | Stripe MCP (community, 500+ stars) | HIGH | Stripe API direct |
| Shopify | Shopify MCP | HIGH | Shopify Admin API |
| Google Sheets | Google Sheets MCP (xing5) | HIGH | Google Workspace MCP |

### Escalation Handler Integrations

| Service | Method | Trust | Fallback |
|---------|--------|-------|----------|
| Slack | Slack MCP Plugin (slackapi, official) | HIGH | Slack API direct |

All integrations are HIGH trust. Shopify and Stripe MCP servers have >500 stars with active maintenance. Slack MCP Plugin is officially maintained by Slack.

context: Integration table with Service and Method columns
Trust scores assigned to integrations

content: All HIGH trust, that is reassuring. Approved.

**Phase 4: Step-by-Step Confirmation + Dry Run | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

Phase 3 approved. Let me present each agent for your confirmation.

### Support Coordinator

- **Actions:** Read new tickets from Shopify inbox and Gmail, classify by type (order-status, refund, escalation), route to appropriate specialist
- **Integrations:** Shopify MCP (read-only), Google Workspace MCP (read-only)
- **Blast Radius:** Level 1 (read-only)
- **Trigger:** Event-driven (polled every 5 minutes)
- **Failure handling:** Retry 3x on API timeout, default to escalation if classification fails

### Order Tracker

- **Actions:** Look up order in Shopify, check fulfillment and shipping status, draft response email, write to state
- **Integrations:** Shopify MCP (read orders), Google Workspace MCP (send email)
- **Blast Radius:** Level 2 (write-scoped to `.agentbloc/state/order-tracker.json`)
- **Trigger:** Routed by Support Coordinator
- **Failure handling:** Retry 3x on Shopify API timeout, escalate if order not found

### Refund Processor

- **Actions:** Check refund eligibility against store policy, calculate refund amount, process via Stripe (under EUR 50) or queue for owner approval (over EUR 50), log to Google Sheets
- **Integrations:** Stripe MCP (create refund), Shopify MCP (read order), Google Sheets MCP (write refund log)
- **Blast Radius:** Level 3 (write-unrestricted, requires approval for all refunds)
- **Trigger:** Routed by Support Coordinator
- **Failure handling:** Never auto-process without confirmation, queue for human review on ambiguity

### Escalation Handler

- **Actions:** Format escalation summary, send to support Slack channel with ticket context, notify owner for high-priority
- **Integrations:** Slack MCP Plugin (send messages)
- **Blast Radius:** Level 4 (send-external, requires approval)
- **Trigger:** Routed by Support Coordinator
- **Failure handling:** Retry 3x on Slack failure, fall back to email notification

All confirmed?

context: Agent card for Support Coordinator presented
Agent card for Refund Processor presented
Blast radius shown in agent cards

content: Confirmed. All four agents look correct. Please run the dry run.

**Phase 4: Step-by-Step Confirmation + Dry Run | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

Running dry run against your last 24 hours of tickets. All side-effect tools are stubbed.

**Dry Run Results:**

Dry run processed 10 sample tickets from the last 24 hours:
- 6 classified as order-status inquiries: Order Tracker drafted responses with correct shipping info
- 3 classified as refund-eligible: Refund Processor evaluated (2 under EUR 50 auto-approved, 1 over EUR 50 queued for owner approval)
- 1 classified as complex: Escalation Handler formatted summary for Slack delivery

All Stripe refund operations and Slack message sends were stubbed and logged. No side effects executed. Classification accuracy looked solid across the sample.

context: Dry run result with processed count

content: What happens if the classifier gets a ticket wrong? Say it classifies a complaint as a simple order inquiry?

**Phase 4: Step-by-Step Confirmation + Dry Run | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

Good question. The system handles misclassification in two ways:

1. **Fallback to escalation:** If the Order Tracker cannot resolve the ticket (e.g., customer is complaining, not asking about status), it flags the ticket and re-routes to the Escalation Handler.
2. **Confidence threshold:** The Support Coordinator assigns a classification confidence score. Below 80%, the ticket goes to Escalation Handler by default rather than risking a wrong specialist.

This means the worst case is a slight delay (ticket gets reclassified), not a wrong response being sent to the customer.

Ready to approve and deploy?
Approved. The classification looks accurate. Let's deploy.

**Phase 5: Deployment | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

Phase 4 approved. Generating deployment artifacts.

```
.agentbloc/

```

**team.yaml excerpt:**

```yaml
name: ecommerce-support
display_name: "E-Commerce Support Automation"
topology: hierarchy
timezone: Europe/Berlin
agents:
  - name: support-coordinator
    role: router
  - name: order-tracker
    role: worker
  - name: refund-processor
    role: worker
  - name: escalation-handler
    role: worker
schedule:
  type: event
  poll_interval: "*/5 * * * *"
```

Credentials in `.env` (gitignored). Refund Processor requires Telegram approval before executing any Stripe refund. Audit logging tracks every classification, refund decision, and escalation with correlation IDs. The governance.yaml enforces PCI-DSS patterns: no full card numbers in logs, PII redaction on all audit entries.

context: Artifact tree showing .agentbloc/ directory
Hierarchy topology in team.yaml config
Governance configuration file referenced

content: Looks complete. Approved.

**Phase 6: Evolution | Gate: pending | Level: technical-basics**

Phase 5 approved. Post-deploy lifecycle management is now active.

The weekly evolution scan checks:

- **Shopify API versions:** Shopify deprecates API versions quarterly; scan for upcoming deprecations
- **Stripe webhook changes:** New event types or payload format changes affecting refund processing
- **MCP server updates:** New versions of Shopify MCP, Stripe MCP, Slack MCP Plugin, Google Workspace MCP
- **Support volume patterns:** If ticket volume exceeds 200/day, recommend scaling the poll interval
- **Security vulnerabilities:** CVE monitoring for all MCP server dependencies

Example patch proposal:

```
Title: Update Shopify MCP to v2.1.0
Priority: P3 MEDIUM
What Changed: Shopify MCP 2.1.0 adds fulfillment tracking event support
Affected Agents: Support Coordinator, Order Tracker
Recommended Action: Update dependency version
Rollback Plan: Revert to Shopify MCP 2.0.x
```

All proposals require explicit human approval. No auto-patching.

context: Evolution scan items listed
Patch proposal format present
Human approval required for all changes

content: Approved. Great work on the full setup.

**Phase 6: Evolution | Gate: approved | Level: technical-basics**

All six phases complete. Your e-commerce support agent team is live:
- **4 agents:** Support Coordinator, Order Tracker, Refund Processor, Escalation Handler
- **Hierarchy topology:** coordinator routes to 3 specialists
- **Compliance:** GDPR + PCI-DSS patterns with PII redaction and audit logging
- **Kill switch:** `.agentbloc/KILL_SWITCH` for emergency stop
- **Evolution:** Weekly scans with human-approved patches only

Your support team can now focus on the 15% of tickets that actually need human judgment.
